---
title: "Resources"
subtitle: "Background reading, software, and related tools"
---

## Survival Analysis

### Textbooks

- Kalbfleisch JD, Prentice RL. *The Statistical Analysis of Failure Time Data.* 2nd ed. Wiley, 2002.
- Klein JP, Moeschberger ML. *Survival Analysis: Techniques for Censored and Truncated Data.* 2nd ed. Springer, 2003.
- Putter H, Fiocco M, Geskus RB. Tutorial in biostatistics: competing risks and multi-state models. *Statistics in Medicine* 2007; 26(11):2389–2430.

### R Packages

| Package | Purpose | CRAN / GitHub |
|---------|---------|---------------|
| `survival` | Core survival analysis | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=survival) |
| `mstate` | Multi-state models | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=mstate) |
| `flexsurv` | Flexible parametric models | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=flexsurv) |
| `frailtyEM` | Frailty models (EM algorithm) | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=frailtyEM) |
| `timereg` | Additive hazard models | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=timereg) |
| `cmprsk` | Competing risks | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=cmprsk) |
| `survminer` | Visualization | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=survminer) |

---

## Infectious Disease Modeling

### Textbooks

- Keeling MJ, Rohani P. *Modeling Infectious Diseases in Humans and Animals.* Princeton University Press, 2008.
- Vynnycky E, White RG. *An Introduction to Infectious Disease Modelling.* Oxford University Press, 2010.
- Anderson RM, May RM. *Infectious Diseases of Humans: Dynamics and Control.* Oxford University Press, 1991.

### R Packages

| Package | Purpose | CRAN / GitHub |
|---------|---------|---------------|
| `EpiEstim` | Rt estimation from case counts | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=EpiEstim) |
| `EpiNow2` | Nowcasting and Rt estimation with delays | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=EpiNow2) |
| `deSolve` | ODE solver for compartmental models | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=deSolve) |
| `odin` | Efficient ODE model specification | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=odin) |
| `socialmixr` | Contact matrices | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=socialmixr) |
| `outbreaks` | Example outbreak datasets | [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=outbreaks) |

---

## Cross-disciplinary Connections

### Key Papers

*Pre-workshop reading papers will be listed here. These will also be distributed to participants directly.*

---

## The Lorentz Center

The [Lorentz Center](https://www.lorentzcenter.nl) in Leiden, the Netherlands, is an international center for scientific workshops. The center facilitates high-impact scientific meetings that stimulate scientific exchange, new collaborations, and innovative research.

This workshop is part of the **NIAS-Lorentz Program**, which brings together perspectives from the humanities and social sciences with the natural and technological sciences.

---

## Questions and Contact

For questions about the workshop, please:

- [Open an issue](https://github.com/kylieainslie/lorentz_center_workshop/issues) on the GitHub repository
- Contact the organizers via the [Lorentz Center workshop page](https://www.lorentzcenter.nl)
